title: Alone One Night
content:
This night,
    Long like the drooping feathers
    Of the pheasant,
    The chain of mountains,
    Shall I sleep alone?

    From the Japanese of Kaik-no Motto-no Hitomaro (seventh and eighth centuries).
meaning:
"Alone One Night" is a quintessential example of Edward Powys Mathers (As Translator)'s signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
